name: Claude Opus 4
tagline: Anthropic's May 2025 flagship — the model it called the world's best for coding and long-horizon agents.
metaDescription: Claude Opus 4 (Anthropic), released 2025-05-22: 200K context, 32K max output, text + vision, $15/$75 per MTok. SWE-bench Verified 72.5%. Specs, benchmarks, pri…
overview: Claude Opus 4 is a large language model from Anthropic, the flagship of the Claude 4 generation released on May 22, 2025. At launch Anthropic positioned Claude Opus 4 as its most capable model and described it as the world's best coding model, built for complex, long-running tasks that can require thousands of steps and several hours of continuous work.

Claude Opus 4 accepts text and image input and returns text, with a 200K-token context window and up to 32K output tokens. It is a hybrid reasoning model that offers both near-instant responses and an extended-thinking mode for deeper, step-by-step reasoning. Pricing at launch was $15 per million input tokens and $75 per million output tokens.

Claude Opus 4 was superseded by Claude Opus 4.1 (August 2025) and later Opus 4.x releases, and the original snapshot (claude-opus-4-20250514) has since been retired. It remains a notable milestone as the model that launched Anthropic's Claude 4 line and its leading position on agentic coding benchmarks.
strengths: Agentic coding — 72.5% on SWE-bench Verified at launch, which Anthropic cited as best-in-class
Sustained long-horizon work — designed to run continuously for hours across thousands of steps
Hybrid reasoning with an extended-thinking mode for harder, multi-step problems
Strong graduate-level reasoning and knowledge (74.9% GPQA Diamond, 87.4% MMLU)
useCases: Large-codebase refactors and multi-file software tasks
Long-running agents that execute many sequential steps

faq.a: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4 on May 22, 2025, as the flagship model of the Claude 4 generation alongside Claude Sonnet 4.

faq.q: What is Claude Opus 4's context window and max output?
faq.a: Claude Opus 4 has a 200K-token context window and can generate up to 32K output tokens per response. It accepts both text and image input.

faq.q: How much did Claude Opus 4 cost?
faq.a: Claude Opus 4 was priced at $15 per million input tokens and $75 per million output tokens via the Anthropic API.

No. The original snapshot (claude-opus-4-20250514) has been retired. It was succeeded by Claude Opus 4.1 in August 2025 and later Opus 4.x models; Anthropic recommends migrating to the current Claude Opus tier.
